knowledge_base: Application Security - GitHub Advanced Security Features

statements: Strong application security (AppSec) improves business outcomes and reduces risk.
Effective code security allows teams to maintain development speed.
Modern security tools integrate directly into developer workflows (especially GitHub).
Two main protection layers: Secret Protection and Code Security (vulnerability scanning).
Secret Protection prevents credential leaks before code reaches the repository (push protection).
Code Security scans for vulnerabilities in real time and provides feedback before deployment.
Scans code, issues, discussions, pull requests, and other repository content for secrets.
Uses validity checks and known public leak data to prioritize real threats over false positives.
GitHub Copilot can detect certain high-risk secrets that pattern-based scanners miss.
GitHub Copilot Autofix provides AI-generated remediation suggestions for detected vulnerabilities.
AI assists in finding secrets with lower false-positive rates than traditional detectors.
Administrators can enforce push protection with limited bypass rights.
Alert dismissal can be restricted to specific roles.
Centralized enablement and policy settings simplify security at scale.
3,000 Actions minutes per month
2 GB GitHub Packages storage
GitHub Codespaces
Protected branches
Multiple reviewers in pull requests
CODEOWNERS file support
Draft pull requests
Required reviewers
GitHub Pages and Wikis
Web-based support
Prevent secret leaks before they enter the repository.
Detect and remediate existing leaked credentials.
Identify and fix code vulnerabilities before production deployment.
